name: Raspberry Chocolate Meringue Icebox Cake
recipeIngredient: 6 oz Fine quality bittersweet chocolate (not unsweetened)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 250°F. Line 2 baking sheets with parchment paper and trace 3 8-inch circles onto the paper.
2. In a large bowl with an electric mixer, beat whites with cream of tartar and add a pinch of salt until they just hold soft peaks. Add 1 cup sugar gradually, beating, and beat meringue until it holds stiff peaks. Transfer meringue to a pastry bag fitted with a 3/4-inch plain tip and pipe onto circles, filling them in. Smooth meringues with a long metal spatula or knife. Alternatively, if the pastry bag isn't used, circles may be made by spreading meringues freehand with a spatula.
3. Bake meringues for 30 minutes and switch positions of baking sheets in the oven. Bake meringues for another 30 minutes, or until pale golden and crisp. (If the weather is humid, cooking time may be longer.) Cool meringues completely and peel off paper. Meringues may be made up to 1 day ahead and kept turned off.
4. In a saucepan, bring 1/4 cup heavy cream just to a boil and remove from heat. Add chocolate and stir mixture until chocolate is melted completely. Divide ganache between 2 meringue layers and gently spread evenly, leaving 1/2-inch borders. Chill ganache until hardened, about 10 minutes.
5. In a small saucepan, sprinkle gelatin over cold water and soften for 1 minute. Heat mixture over low heat, stirring until gelatin is dissolved, and keep warm. In a bowl with an electric mixer, beat remaining 2 3/4 cups heavy cream with remaining 1/2 cup sugar until it just holds stiff peaks. Then beat in sour cream and vanilla. Add gelatin mixture in a stream, beating, and beat until the mixture holds stiff peaks.
6. Arrange 1 ganache-topped meringue on a plate and spread with 1 1/2 cups cream mixture, mounding at the edge. Arrange 1 pint of raspberries over the cream mixture and top with the second ganache-topped meringue. Repeat procedure with about 1 1/2 cups more cream mixture and another pint of raspberries and top with the third meringue.
7. Transfer about 1 cup more cream mixture to a pastry bag fitted with a star tip and reserve for decoration. Frost top and side of cake with remaining cream mixture and decorate with reserved mixture. Chill cake for at least 2 hours, up to 8 hours. Remove wax paper carefully and scatter the remaining pint of raspberries around and on the cake. Cut into 12 wedges with a serrated or electric knife.
